title: narcissus × biflorus var. sterilis
intro: Narcissus × biflorus var. sterilis, also known as the sterile daffodil, is a variant of the narcissus biflorus plant, characterized by its lack of functional reproductive organs, making it sterile. This plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, due to the presence of alkaloids such as galantamine and lycorine. The health benefits of Narcissus × biflorus var. sterilis include its potential to reduce anxiety and improve cognitive function, as well as its antimicrobial properties, which can aid in wound healing. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in traditional medicine, and its extracts are used in homeopathic remedies and supplements, although its use should be strictly regulated due to the presence of toxic compounds.
